queries: وَلَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ

output.code: import json, ast, textwrap, re, itertools, os, math, datetime

# Load quranic_verses.json
import json, gzip, io, os, re, textwrap, sys, pathlib, math

# We'll inspect the 'quranic_verses.json' file path from results after reading path; file referencing path could be /mnt/data/quranic_verses.json maybe.
path = '/mnt/data/quranic_verses.json'
print(os.path.exists(path))
import re, json, pprint, itertools, unicodedata, math

# We'll find all quoted Arabic segments inside double quotes in the text.
quote_pattern = re.compile(r'\"([^\"]+)\"')
quoted = list(quote_pattern.finditer(text))
len(quoted)

for i, m in enumerate(quoted, 1):
    print(i, m.group(1)[:120])
print(text[-500:])
print(text.rfind('"'))

pos = text.rfind('"')
print(pos, text[pos:pos+100])

quotes = []
for m in quoted:
    start, end = m.span(1)
    quotes.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
# unmatched last quote
pos = text.rfind('"')
if pos != -1 and pos == text.rfind('"') and pos > quotes[-1][1]:  # this ensures unmatched
    start = pos+1
    end = len(text)
    quotes.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
len(quotes)

for idx, (start, end, q) in enumerate(quotes, 1):
    print(idx, start, end, q[:120])

canonical_13 = "وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ"
canonical_18 = "وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ"
len(canonical_13), len(canonical_18)

citations = []
for idx, (start, end, text_span) in enumerate(quotes, 1):
    obj = {
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": text_span,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": "لقمان",
            "sura_number": 31,
            "aya_number": 13 if idx in (1, 2) else 18
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": canonical_13 if idx in (1, 2) else canonical_18,
        "completely_invalid": False
    }
    # For last (#15) maybe truncated; but we still have canonical_18. good.
    citations.append(obj)
len(citations)

# We'll assert our canonical_18 matches
print(canonical_18 == canonical_18_file)

canonical_13_file = "وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ"
print(canonical_13 == canonical_13_file)

output = {"citations": citations}
import json, sys, textwrap, os, pprint, re, math

json_output = json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
print(json_output[:1000])
print(json_output)

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
